2026-04-07T20:27:06.9734399Z 
2026-04-07T20:27:08.2162306Z **Gaps identified** in `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core` (triggered by PR #57877 by @needuv):
2026-04-07T20:27:09.3844649Z 1. README `Telemetry` section doesn't document `FoundryEnrichmentProcessor`, the four OTel tags it stamps, or the overwrite-resistance design (why tags go in `OnEnd`)
2026-04-07T20:27:09.3848299Z 2. `FoundryEnvironment` section doesn't explain env var → OTel attribute mapping
2026-04-07T20:27:09.7877834Z 3. CHANGELOG is missing an entry for this behavioral change
2026-04-07T20:27:09.7913314Z 4. No sample covers the Foundry telemetry enrichment scenario

2026-04-07T20:27:13.4921055Z   6. Contributing ✅
2026-04-07T20:27:13.4921300Z   
2026-04-07T20:27:13.4923047Z   The main change in this commit moves `gen_ai.agent.name`, `gen_ai.agent.version`, and `gen_ai.agent.id` from the `OnStart` phase to `OnEnd` in `FoundryEnrichmentProcessor`, which means these telemetry tags now take precedence over underlying framework values. I should check if the README's Telemetry section needs updating to reflect this behavioral change.
2026-04-07T20:27:13.4924973Z   
2026-04-07T20:27:13.4927616Z   Looking at what's currently documented, the Telemetry section explains how OpenTelemetry is configured through `Azure.Monitor.OpenTelemetry.AspNetCore` and mentions the activity source and meter names, but it's missing details about `FoundryEnrichmentProcessor` itself, the specific agent identity tags it adds, and the significance of when those tags get applied.
2026-04-07T20:27:13.4929792Z   
2026-04-07T20:27:13.4932147Z   The next thinking identifies several documentation gaps: the README doesn't explain `FoundryEnrichmentProcessor` or its tags, the CHANGELOG is missing an entry for this behavioral change, and the `FoundryEnvironment` section doesn't map environment variables to their corresponding telemetry tags. This is a meaningful change that deserves proper documentation, so I should file an issue to track these gaps.

2026-04-07T20:27:13.4941081Z   
2026-04-07T20:27:13.4941398Z   1. **README Telemetry section** is missing:
2026-04-07T20:27:13.4941950Z      - Any mention of `FoundryEnrichmentProcessor` and what it does
2026-04-07T20:27:13.4943037Z      - The specific OpenTelemetry tags it sets: `gen_ai.agent.name`, `gen_ai.agent.version`, `gen_ai.agent.id`, `microsoft.foundry.project.id`
2026-04-07T20:27:13.4944594Z      - The behavior that agent identity tags are set in `OnEnd` (not `OnStart`) to ensure they take precedence over underlying frameworks (e.g., OpenAI SDK)
2026-04-07T20:27:13.4946247Z      - How the environment variables `FOUNDRY_AGENT_NAME`, `FOUNDRY_AGENT_VERSION`, and `FOUNDRY_PROJECT_ARM_ID` drive these telemetry values
2026-04-07T20:27:13.4947146Z   
2026-04-07T20:27:13.4948129Z   2. **README FoundryEnvironment section** mentions those same environment variables but doesn't explain their connection to the telemetry tags that get set downstream.
2026-04-07T20:27:13.4949198Z   
2026-04-07T20:27:13.4950209Z   3. **CHANGELOG** for 1.0.0-beta.21 is missing an entry documenting this behavioral change where the enrichment processor defers agent tag assignment to `OnEnd` for proper precedence.
2026-04-07T20:27:13.4951342Z   
2026-04-07T20:27:13.4952942Z   4. **No documentation or examples** show how to set up Foundry telemetry enrichment end-to-end — configuring the agent identity variables and what the resulting OTel spans actually look like. I should open an issue to track this gap.

2026-04-07T20:27:13.6159742Z [2026-04-07T20:24:51Z] [DEBUG] [rpc] safeoutputs←resp 6468b {"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":1,"result":{"tools":[{"description":"Create a new GitHub issue for tracking bugs, feature requests, or tasks. Use this for actionable work items that need assignment, labeling, and status tracking. For reports, announcements, or status updates that don't require task tracking, use create_discussion instead. CONSTRAINTS: Maximum 1 issue(s) can be created.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"body":{"description":"Detailed issue description in Markdown. Do NOT repeat the title as a heading since it already appears as the issue's h1. Include context, reproduction steps, or acceptance criteria as appropriate.","type":"string"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"labels":{"description":"Labels to categorize the issue (e.g., 'bug', 'enhancement'). Labels must exist in the repository.","items":{"type":"string"},"type":"array"},"parent":{"description":"Parent issue number for creating sub-issues. This is the numeric ID from the GitHub URL (e.g., 42 in github.com/owner/repo/issues/42). Can also be a temporary_id (e.g., 'aw_abc123', 'aw_Test123') from a previously created issue in the same workflow run.","type":["number","string"]},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"},"temporary_id":{"description":"Unique temporary identifier for referencing this issue before it's created. Format: 'aw_' followed by 3 to 12 alphanumeric characters (e.g., 'aw_abc1', 'aw_Test123'). Use '#aw_ID' in body text to reference other issues by their temporary_id; these are replaced with actual issue numbers after creation.","pattern":"^aw_[A-Za-z0-9]{3,12}$","type":"string"},"title":{"description":"Concise issue title summarizing the bug, feature, or task. The title appears as the main heading, so keep it brief and descriptive.","type":"string"}},"required":["title","body"],"type":"object"},"name":"create_issue"},{"description":"Report that a tool or capability needed to complete the task is not available, or share any information you deem important about missing functionality or limitations. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because the required functionality is missing or access is restricted.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"alternatives":{"description":"Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"reason":{"description":"Explanation of why this tool is needed or what information you want to share about the limitation (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"},"tool":{"description":"Optional: Name or description of the missing tool or capability (max 128 characters). Be specific about what functionality is needed.","type":"string"}},"required":["reason"],"type":"object"},"name":"missing_tool"},{"description":"Log a transparency message when no significant actions are needed. Use this to confirm workflow completion and provide visibility when analysis is complete but no changes or outputs are required (e.g., 'No issues found', 'All checks passed'). This ensures the workflow produces human-visible output even when no other actions are taken.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"message":{"description":"Status or completion message to log. Should explain what was analyzed and the outcome (e.g., 'Code review complete - no issues found', 'Analysis complete - all tests passing').","type":"string"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"}},"required":["message"],"type":"object"},"name":"noop"},{"description":"Report that data or information needed to complete the task is not available. Use this when you cannot accomplish what was requested because required data, context, or information is missing.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"alternatives":{"description":"Any workarounds, manual steps, or alternative approaches the user could take (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"context":{"description":"Additional context about the missing data or where it should come from (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"data_type":{"description":"Type or description of the missing data or information (max 128 characters). Be specific about what data is needed.","type":"string"},"integrity":{"description":"Trustworthiness level of the message source (e.g., \"low\", \"medium\", \"high\").","type":"string"},"reason":{"description":"Explanation of why this data is needed to complete the task (max 256 characters).","type":"string"},"secrecy":{"description":"Confidentiality level of the message content (e.g., \"public\", \"internal\", \"private\").","type":"string"}},"required":[],"type":"object"},"name":"missing_data"},{"description":"Signal that the task could not be completed due to an infrastructure or tool failure (e.g., MCP server crash, missing authentication, inaccessible repository). Use this when required tools or data are unavailable and the task cannot be meaningfully performed. This is distinct from noop (no action needed) — it indicates an active failure that prevented the task from running. The workflow framework will treat this as a failure signal even when the agent exits successfully.","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{"details":{"description":"Optional extended details or diagnostic context about the failure (max 65000 characters).","type":"string"},"reason":{"description":"A concise explanation of why the task could not be completed (max 1024 characters). Be specific about which tools or resources were unavailable.","type":"string"}},"required":["reason"],"type":"object"},"name":"report_incomplete"},{"description":"Dispatch the issue triage workflow for the newly created issue","inputSchema":{"additionalProperties":false,"properties":{},"type":"object"},"name":"dispatch_triage"}]}}

2026-04-07T20:27:13.6921457Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-07T20:25:40.085152208Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"successfully downloaded text file (SHA: [REDACTED])","type":"text"},{"resource":{"mimeType":"text/plain; charset=utf-8","text":"// Copyright (c) Microsoft Corporation. All rights reserved.\n// Licensed under the MIT License.\n\nusing System.Diagnostics;\nusing OpenTelemetry;\n\nnamespace Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core.Internal;\n\n/// \u003csummary\u003e\n/// An OpenTelemetry span processor that enriches every span with Foundry\n/// agent identity and project attributes. This processor is automatically\n/// registered by \u003csee cref=\"OpenTelemetryExtensions.AddAgentHostTelemetry\"/\u003e\n/// so that all protocol packages receive the enrichment without additional\n/// configuration.\n/// \u003c/summary\u003e\n/// \u003cremarks\u003e\n/// The processor reads \u003cc\u003eFOUNDRY_AGENT_NAME\u003c/c\u003e, \u003cc\u003eFOUNDRY_AGENT_VERSION\u003c/c\u003e,\n/// and \u003cc\u003eFOUNDRY_PROJECT_ARM_ID\u003c/c\u003e once at construction time and stamps\n/// them onto every started \u003csee cref=\"Activity\"/\u003e.\n/// \u003c/remarks\u003e\ninternal sealed class FoundryEnrichmentProcessor : BaseProcessor\u003cActivity\u003e\n{\n    private readonly string? _agentName;\n    private readonly string? _agentVersion;\n    private readonly string? _agentId;\n    private readonly string? _projectId;\n\n    public FoundryEnrichmentProcessor()\n    {\n        _agentName = FoundryEnvironment.AgentName;\n        _agentVersion = FoundryEnvironment.AgentVersion;\n        _projectId = FoundryEnvironment.ProjectArmId;\n\n        _agentId = _agentName is not null \u0026\u0026 _agentVersion is not null\n            ? $\"{_agentName}:{_agentVersion}\"\n            : _agentName;\n    }\n\n    /// \u003cinheritdoc/\u003e\n    public override void OnStart(Activity activity)\n    {\n        if (_projectId is not null)\n        {\n            activity.SetTag(\"microsoft.foundry.project.id\", _projectId);\n        }\n    }\n\n    /// \u003cinheritdoc/\u003e\n    /// \u003cremarks\u003e\n    /// Agent identity tags are set in \u003cc\u003eOnEnd\u003c/c\u003e rather than \u003cc\u003eOnStart\u003c/c\u003e\n    /// so that they take precedence over any values an underlying framework\n    /// (e.g. the OpenAI SDK) may have stamped during the span's lifetime.\n    /// \u003c/remarks\u003e\n    public override void OnEnd(Activity activity)\n    {\n        if (_agentName is not null)\n        {\n            activity.SetTag(\"gen_ai.agent.name\", _agentName);\n        }\n\n        if (_agentVersion is not null)\n        {\n            activity.SetTag(\"gen_ai.agent.version\", _agentVersion);\n        }\n\n        if (_agentId is not null)\n        {\n            activity.SetTag(\"gen_ai.agent.id\", _agentId);\n        }\n    }\n}\n","uri":"repo://Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/sha/[REDACTED]/contents/sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core/src/Telemetry/FoundryEnrichmentProcessor.cs"},"type":"resource"}]}}}
